note: The first end-to-end percent-of-oracle, and the honesty anchor of the whole sweep. LRU at low pressure lands near the offline optimum, exactly as the pre-registered eval predicts for the regime where policy should not matter (the Scenario-5 control property), confirmed on real Claude Code traces before the grid was pinned. When lifecycle policies later win under pressure, this number is the other half of the story: the benchmark does not manufacture wins where theory says there are none.

config._ratio_note: relabeled to cap/T; denominator = measured distinct tokens T=193,728 (2-session s_0002+s_0700, block64); earlier 1.4 used a looser denominator; run config unchanged. Note this is a DIFFERENT corpus/granularity than the 11-session ladder, so its cap/T is on its own scale.

caveat: Low-pressure regime only. The interesting high-pressure, many-session regime hits the O(resident)-per-eviction scalability wall; the granularity coarsening (block_tokens 64 -> 256) is the first-order fix before the ladder runs.
